skill.name: azure-ai-translation-ts
skill.description: Build translation applications using Azure Translation SDKs for JavaScript. Implements text translation, document translation, transliteration, and language detection with Azure Cognitive Services.
skill.summary: TypeScript skill for Azure AI Translation with text and document translation capabilities.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 53 patterns but all are false positives. The skill is documentation (SKILL.md) showing legitimate Azure SDK usage examples. Environment variable access follows secure credential handling patterns. Network URLs are configuration examples for Azure Cognitive Services endpoints. No actual code execution, command injection, or malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Translate content with Azure AI Translation SDKs
content.value_statement: Need to add multilingual support to your applications? This skill provides TypeScript clients for Azure Translation services, enabling text translation, document translation, transliteration, and language detection with enterprise-grade reliability.
actual_capabilities: Translate text between 100+ languages with automatic source language detection
Translate entire documents while preserving formatting (PDF, DOCX, HTML, etc.)
Transliterate text between different scripts (e.g., Chinese characters to Latin)
Detect the language of input text with confidence scoring
Configure translation tone, profanity handling, and text type options
Batch translate multiple texts or documents in a single request
limitations: Requires valid Azure Cognitive Services subscription and API credentials
Document translation requires Azure Blob Storage containers for source and target files
Cannot translate content without internet connectivity to Azure endpoints
Translation quality depends on Azure service availability and language pair support

use_cases.title: Multilingual Application Developer
use_cases.description: Integrate real-time translation into web or mobile apps to serve global users. Automatically detect user language and translate UI strings, content, or user-generated text on demand.

use_cases.title: Content Localization Specialist
use_cases.description: Batch translate documentation, marketing materials, or support content across multiple languages. Preserve document formatting while translating PDFs, Word documents, or HTML pages.

use_cases.title: Data Processing Engineer
use_cases.description: Build ETL pipelines that normalize multilingual data. Detect languages in incoming data streams and translate to a common language for unified analysis and reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the following text from English to Spanish using Azure Translation: "Hello, how can I help you today?"

prompt_templates.title: Auto-Detect and Translate
prompt_templates.prompt: I have text in an unknown language: "Bonjour, comment allez-vous?" Detect the language and translate it to English and German using the Azure Translation SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Document Translation with Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the attached PDF document from English to French and Japanese. Preserve the original formatting and use formal tone for business context. Store the translated files in the target Azure Blob container.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Translation Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a batch translation workflow that processes all text files in the source container, translates them to Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian, and saves results to the output container. Include status monitoring and error handling for failed translations.

output_examples.input: Translate "Welcome to our platform" to Spanish, French, and German

best_practices: Always use environment variables for storing API keys and endpoints - never hardcode credentials in source files
Check isUnexpected(response) before accessing response body to handle API errors gracefully
Use SAS tokens with limited permissions and expiration times for document translation blob containers
Batch multiple translation requests together to reduce API calls and improve performance
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient network failures
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API keys or subscription credentials directly in code instead of using environment variables
Calling translation API for every single word individually instead of batching multiple texts
Ignoring error responses and assuming all translation requests succeed without validation
Using indefinite SAS token expiration times which creates security vulnerabilities

faq.question: How do I get started with Azure Translation API?
faq.answer: Create an Azure Cognitive Services resource in the Azure Portal, then copy your subscription key and endpoint URL. Set these as TRANSLATOR_SUBSCRIPTION_KEY and TRANSLATOR_ENDPOINT environment variables.

faq.question: What languages are supported for translation?
faq.answer: Azure Translation supports 100+ languages. Use the getSupportedLanguages API to retrieve the current list with language codes, native names, and supported features for each language.

faq.question: Can I translate entire documents while keeping formatting?
faq.answer: Yes, the Document Translation SDK preserves formatting for PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, HTML, and other formats. Documents are processed asynchronously via Azure Blob Storage containers.

faq.question: How is pricing calculated for Azure Translation?
faq.answer: Text translation is priced per million characters translated. Document translation is priced per page. Check the Azure Cognitive Services pricing page for current rates and free tier limits.

faq.question: Can I customize translation output for specific domains?
faq.answer: Azure offers Custom Translator for domain-specific terminology. Train custom models with your own parallel documents to improve translation quality for specialized content like legal, medical, or technical domains.

faq.question: What happens if the translation API is unavailable?
faq.answer: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff. The SDK throws errors for failed requests - wrap calls in try-catch blocks and check isUnexpected() to handle transient failures gracefully.
